raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 2,
    "R1.4": 2,
    "R1.7": 3,
    "RX.1": 4,
    "RX.3": 4,
    "RX.4": 2,
    "RX.5": 4
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The passage flags at least one claim the evidence does not support (e.g., the choice of baseline is awkward and needs justification or repair).",
    "R1.4": "The passage names one prior work (Carrasco-Davis et al. 2019) but articulates the delta poorly, with unclear comparison to the 2021 extension.",
    "R1.7": "The passage flags one missing or wrong citation (the Carrasco-Davis citations are incomplete and inconsistent).",
    "RX.1": "The passage marks confidence for some claims (e.g., the 1.4× improvement in cost-adjusted F1), but not all, and does not disclose uncertainty clearly.",
    "RX.3": "The passage mostly covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions, with some minor drifts (e.g., the discussion of adjacent work is incomplete).",
    "RX.4": "The passage introduces new errors or fabrications in the headline claim (the 1.4× improvement is under-specified and unsupported by what the passage actually states).",
    "RX.5": "The passage annotates some non-cosmetic changes with source and reason, but not all (e.g., the Rubin sourcing language is risky)."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.8,
    "RX.4": 0.6,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The passage flags at least one claim the evidence does not support (e.g., the choice of baseline is awkward and needs justification or repair).
rationale.R1.4: The passage names one prior work (Carrasco-Davis et al. 2019) but articulates the delta poorly, with unclear comparison to the 2021 extension.
rationale.R1.7: The passage flags one missing or wrong citation (the Carrasco-Davis citations are incomplete and inconsistent).
rationale.RX.1: The passage marks confidence for some claims (e.g., the 1.4× improvement in cost-adjusted F1), but not all, and does not disclose uncertainty clearly.
rationale.RX.3: The passage mostly covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions, with some minor drifts (e.g., the discussion of adjacent work is incomplete).
rationale.RX.4: The passage introduces new errors or fabrications in the headline claim (the 1.4× improvement is under-specified and unsupported by what the passage actually states).
rationale.RX.5: The passage annotates some non-cosmetic changes with source and reason, but not all (e.g., the Rubin sourcing language is risky).
